7. **Post-mortem** — Written within 48 hours (P1) or 1 week (P2); blameless format; includes timeline, root cause, action items

**Runbooks Maintained For:**
- Database failover procedure
- Redis cache eviction / memory pressure
- Twilio call routing degradation
- Claude API rate limiting / fallback to cached responses
- CDN cache invalidation
- Deployment rollback procedure

---

### 28.5 Data Backup and Disaster Recovery

**Backup Policy:**
- PostgreSQL: Automated daily snapshots (RDS), 30-day retention; point-in-time recovery to any second within 7 days
- S3 call recordings: Cross-region replication to us-west-2; versioning enabled
- Redis: AOF persistence + RDB snapshots every 15 minutes; 3-day retention
- Terraform state: Stored in S3 with versioning and MFA delete protection

**Disaster Recovery Targets:**
- RTO (Recovery Time Objective): < 2 hours for full service restoration
- RPO (Recovery Point Objective): < 15 minutes data loss maximum

**DR Test Schedule:** Quarterly DR drill — simulate primary region failure, verify failover to standby, restore from backup, confirm service restoration within RTO. Results documented in `ops/dr-test-log.md`.

**Multi-Region Strategy (Year 2):**
At 1,000+ customers, stand up active-passive DR in us-west-2. Route53 health-check failover. Target < 5 minute automated failover with zero data loss for Studio tier customers.

---

### 28.6 Support Tier Structure

**Self-Serve (All Tiers):**
- Help center at docs.ringdesk.ai — searchable, 200+ articles
- In-app onboarding wizard with step-by-step guidance
- Video tutorials for all core features (< 3 min each)
- Community forum at community.ringdesk.ai
- AI-powered support bot for common questions (RingDesk dogfoods own product)

**Email Support (All Tiers):**
- Response SLA: Starter < 48 hours, Pro < 8 hours, Studio < 1 hour
- Support ticketing via Intercom; routing by tier automatic
- First contact resolution target: 75%

**Dedicated CSM (Studio Tier Only):**
- Named Customer Success Manager assigned at onboarding
- Monthly business review call (30 min)
- Direct Slack channel with CSM and engineering escalation path
- Proactive outreach when usage patterns indicate churn risk (ML-flagged)
- Quarterly product roadmap briefing with input on prioritization

**Head of Customer Success (Month 9 Hire):**
- Owns Studio tier relationships and expansion revenue
- Builds CS playbook, onboarding materials, health scoring model
- Reports to CEO; bridges product and sales
- Hiring criteria: 3+ years CS leadership at SaaS company, familiarity with SMB segment, data-driven approach to health scoring

---

### 28.7 Key Operational Hires

**Month 6 — VP Engineering**
- Full ownership of engineering org, hiring, and technical roadmap
- Frees CTO for architecture, AI strategy, and vendor negotiations
- Target: 2+ prior direct reports, shipped production SaaS at Series A+ stage

**Month 9 — Head of Customer Success**
- Owns Studio tier, post-sale expansion, and churn prevention
- Builds the CS function from scratch; first CS hire after this role is month 12

**Month 10 — Security Engineer (Part-Time → Full-Time at Month 14)**
- Leads SOC 2 Type II audit preparation (targeting certification by Month 18)
- Owns vulnerability management, penetration testing schedule, security incident response

**Month 12 — Data Engineer**
- Builds analytics data warehouse (Snowflake or BigQuery)
- Powers internal business intelligence + customer-facing analytics features
- Enables cohort analysis, predictive churn models, LTV segmentation at scale

---

**Reliability Metrics (Measured Weekly):**
| Metric | Target | Alert Threshold |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| API uptime | ≥ 99.9% | < 99.5% |
| p99 latency | < 400ms | > 600ms |
| Call answer time | < 800ms | > 1,200ms |
| Error rate (5xx) | < 0.1% | > 0.5% |
| Deployment frequency | ≥ 3/week | < 1/week |
| MTTR (P1 incidents) | < 30 min | > 60 min |

**Customer Operations Metrics (Measured Monthly):**
| Metric | Target | Alert Threshold |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| Support ticket resolution | < 4 hours (Pro) | > 8 hours |
| First contact resolution | ≥ 75% | < 60% |
| Monthly churn rate | < 3% | > 5% |
| Net Revenue Retention | > 105% | < 100% |
| CSAT score | ≥ 4.4 / 5.0 | < 4.0 |
| Onboarding completion rate | ≥ 85% | < 70% |

**Engineering Velocity Metrics:**
| Metric | Target | Note |
|--------|--------|------|
| Deploy cycle time | < 8 minutes | Lint → test → prod |
| Test coverage | ≥ 80% | Enforced via CI gate |
| P1 incidents per month | ≤ 1 | Track in post-mortem log |
| Tech debt ratio | < 20% sprint capacity | Reviewed in weekly planning |
| Open CVEs (critical) | 0 | Patched within 24h of disclosure |

## Section 29: Financial Projections and Unit Economics

### 29.1 Three-Year Revenue Projection

**Core Assumptions:**
- Average Revenue Per Account (ARPA): $148/month blended (Starter: $49, Pro: $149, Studio: $249 — mix 20/60/20 by Year 2)
- Monthly churn: 4% in Year 1, improving to 2.8% Year 2, 2.2% Year 3
- Trial-to-paid conversion: 28% Year 1, 33% Year 2, 38% Year 3
- New customer acquisition: primarily SDR-led Year 1, shifting to product-led/partner Year 2–3

**Annual Recurring Revenue:**

| Period | Customers (EOP) | ARPA | ARR | Growth |
|--------|----------------|------|-----|--------|
| Year 1 (Month 12) | 243 | $148 | $432K | — |
| Year 2 (Month 24) | 1,215 | $148 | $2.16M | 5× |
| Year 3 (Month 36) | 3,000 | $180 | $6.48M | 3× |

ARPA increases to $180 by Year 3 as Studio tier penetration grows and per-seat pricing is introduced for multi-staff operations.

**Monthly Revenue Build (Year 1):**

| Month | New Customers | Churned | Net New | Cumulative | MRR |
|-------|--------------|---------|---------|------------|-----|
| 1 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 8 | $1.2K |
| 2 | 12 | 1 | 11 | 19 | $2.8K |
| 3 | 18 | 1 | 17 | 36 | $5.3K |
| 4 | 22 | 2 | 20 | 56 | $8.3K |
| 5 | 28 | 2 | 26 | 82 | $12.1K |
| 6 | 32 | 3 | 29 | 111 | $16.4K |
| 7 | 35 | 5 | 30 | 141 | $20.9K |
| 8 | 38 | 6 | 32 | 173 | $25.6K |
| 9 | 40 | 7 | 33 | 206 | $30.5K |
| 10 | 42 | 8 | 34 | 240 | $35.5K |
| 11 | 44 | 8 | 36 | 276 | $40.8K |
| 12 | 45 | 9 | 36 | 312 | $46.2K |

**Cumulative ARR at Month 12: ~$432K (annualizing Month 12 MRR × 12 × net retention)**

---

### 29.2 Revenue Mix and Tier Distribution

**Target Tier Mix by Year:**

| Tier | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 |
|------|--------|--------|--------|
| Starter ($49) | 35% | 20% | 15% |
| Pro ($149) | 55% | 60% | 55% |
| Studio ($249) | 10% | 20% | 30% |

Revenue concentration risk: Pro tier dominates, which is healthy — it's the "just right" tier that maximizes LTV without requiring enterprise sales motion. Studio tier growth in Year 2–3 is driven by multi-location expansion and franchise system deals.

**Expansion Revenue (Net Revenue Retention):**
- Year 1: NRR = 98% (churn slightly exceeds expansion)
- Year 2: NRR = 107% (expansion from Starter→Pro upgrades exceeds churn)
- Year 3: NRR = 112% (Studio upsells + per-seat growth from multi-location accounts)

---

### 29.3 Unit Economics — LTV / CAC Model

**Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC):**

| Channel | CAC | % of Mix (Yr 1) | Blended Contribution |
|---------|-----|----------------|---------------------|
| Direct sales (SDR outreach) | $220 | 55% | $121 |
| Paid digital (Meta/Google) | $195 | 25% | $49 |
| Partner referral | $85 | 10% | $9 |
| Organic/inbound | $45 | 10% | $5 |
| **Blended CAC** | **$184** | 100% | **$184** |

By Year 2, blended CAC improves to $155 as organic and partner channels scale.

**Customer Lifetime Value (LTV):**

| Scenario | ARPA | Gross Margin | Monthly Churn | LTV |
|----------|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Year 1 (base) | $148 | 65% | 4.0% | $2,405 |
| Year 2 (improved) | $148 | 70% | 2.8% | $3,700 |
| Year 3 (scale) | $180 | 72% | 2.2% | $5,891 |

*LTV = (ARPA × Gross Margin %) / Monthly Churn Rate*

**LTV:CAC Ratio:**

| Period | LTV | CAC | Ratio | Target |
|--------|-----|-----|-------|--------|
| Year 1 | $2,405 | $184 | 13.1× | ≥ 3× ✓ |
| Year 2 | $3,700 | $155 | 23.9× | ≥ 3× ✓ |
| Year 3 | $5,891 | $120 | 49.1× | ≥ 3× ✓ |

Even at Year 1 churn rates, LTV:CAC exceeds 3× by a wide margin — indicating strong unit economics and room for increased acquisition spend.

**CAC Payback Period:**

| Period | ARPA × Gross Margin | CAC | Payback (months) |
|--------|--------------------|----|-----------------|
| Year 1 | $148 × 65% = $96/mo | $184 | 1.9 months |
| Year 2 | $148 × 70% = $104/mo | $155 | 1.5 months |
| Year 3 | $180 × 72% = $130/mo | $120 | 0.9 months |

Sub-2-month payback in Year 1 is exceptional for SaaS — a function of low CAC in SMB direct sales and relatively high ARPA vs. price point. This is the key investor talking point.

---

### 29.4 Gross Margin Structure

**Variable Cost of Revenue per Customer (Monthly):**

| Cost Item | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 | Notes |
|-----------|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| AI API (Claude) | $8.50 | $6.20 | $4.80 | Volume discount at scale |
| Twilio (calls + SMS) | $12.00 | $9.50 | $7.50 | Per-minute costs; negotiated at scale |
| Infrastructure (AWS) | $4.50 | $3.20 | $2.40 | ECS, RDS, S3 per customer share |
| Deepgram STT | $3.20 | $2.40 | $1.80 | Volume tier |
| ElevenLabs TTS | $2.80 | $2.10 | $1.60 | Volume tier |
| **Total COGS** | **$31.00** | **$23.40** | **$18.10** | |

**Gross Margin:**

| Period | ARPA | COGS | Gross Profit | GM% |
|--------|------|------|-------------|-----|
| Year 1 | $148 | $31 | $117 | 79% |
| Year 2 | $148 | $23 | $125 | 85% |
| Year 3 | $180 | $18 | $162 | 90% |

*Note: Actual reported gross margin will be lower (65–72%) once support costs and customer success are allocated to COGS — a common SaaS accounting choice. Pure product gross margin (excluding CS) targets 85%+ by Year 2.*

Key COGS driver in Year 1 is Twilio telephony costs. As call volume grows and a direct carrier relationship is established (Year 3), per-minute costs decline by ~35%.

---

### 29.5 Operating Expense Model and Burn Rate

**Headcount Plan and Fully-Loaded Cost:**

| Period | Headcount | Avg Fully-Loaded Cost | Total Payroll |
|--------|-----------|----------------------|---------------|
| Month 1–6 (Seed) | 5 | $165K/yr | $69K/mo |
| Month 7–12 (Seed+) | 9 | $165K/yr | $124K/mo |
| Month 13–24 (Series A) | 18 | $175K/yr | $263K/mo |

**Full Operating Expense Breakdown (Monthly):**

| Category | Month 6 | Month 12 | Month 24 |
|----------|---------|---------|---------|
| Payroll + benefits | $69K | $124K | $263K |
| Infrastructure (AWS) | $2.5K | $8K | $28K |
| AI APIs (Twilio, Claude, Deepgram) | $4K | $16K | $58K |
| Sales & marketing | $22K | $48K | $95K |
| Office + G&A | $8K | $12K | $22K |
| Legal + compliance | $5K | $8K | $15K |
| **Total OpEx** | **$110K** | **$216K** | **$481K** |

**Monthly Burn Rate:**

| Phase | Gross Revenue | Total OpEx | Net Burn |
|-------|--------------|-----------|---------|
| Month 6 | $16K | $110K | -$94K |
| Month 12 | $46K | $216K | -$170K |
| Month 18 | $130K | $310K | -$180K |
| Month 24 | $180K | $380K | -$200K |
| Month 30 | $310K | $310K | $0 (breakeven) |

**Seed Round Sizing:** $1.5M seed finances 15 months of operations at -$100K/month average burn, targeting Series A raise at Month 12 with $432K ARR as the fundraising milestone.

**Series A Sizing:** $6M Series A finances 24 months of operations at -$250K/month average post-Series-A burn, targeting Series B or profitability at Month 36.

---

### 29.6 Path to Profitability

**Breakeven Analysis:**
- Breakeven MRR required: ~$310K (operating costs at month 30 headcount)
- Customers required at $148 ARPA: ~2,100 customers
- Timeline: Month 30 (18 months post-Series A)

**Profitability Milestones:**

| Milestone | Target Date | Signal |
|-----------|------------|--------|
| Unit economics positive | Month 4 | CAC payback < 3 months confirmed |
| Gross profit > $0 | Month 3 | Revenue > COGS |
| Contribution margin positive | Month 14 | Revenue > COGS + variable S&M |
| Operating breakeven | Month 30 | Revenue > all OpEx |
| GAAP profitability | Month 36 | Accounting for D&A and SBC |

---

### 29.7 Sensitivity Analysis

**Scenario Matrix — ARR at Month 24:**

| Scenario | Monthly Churn | Trial Conv. | CAC | ARR (Mo 24) |
|----------|--------------|------------|-----|------------|
| Bear | 6.0% | 22% | $240 | $980K |
| Base | 2.8% | 33% | $155 | $2.16M |
| Bull | 1.5% | 42% | $110 | $4.20M |

**Churn Sensitivity on LTV (Year 1 ARPA = $148, GM = 65%):**

| Monthly Churn | LTV | LTV:CAC | Viable? |
|--------------|-----|---------|---------|
| 2% | $4,810 | 26× | Strong |
| 3% | $3,207 | 17× | Good |
| 4% | $2,405 | 13× | OK |
| 6% | $1,603 | 8.7× | Marginal |
| 8% | $1,203 | 6.5× | Watch |
| 12% | $802 | 4.4× | Concerning |

Even at 8% monthly churn, LTV:CAC exceeds 3× threshold — giving confidence that the business is viable even in a bear scenario. Primary risk to unit economics is ARPA compression (price sensitivity) more than churn rate.

---

### 29.8 Key Financial Assumptions and Risks

**Assumptions:**
1. AI API costs (Claude, Deepgram, ElevenLabs) decline 25–35% by Year 3 via volume discounts and model efficiency improvements
2. Twilio per-minute costs decline from $0.0085 to $0.0055 by Year 3 via direct carrier negotiation at 1M+ minutes/month
3. Average calls handled per customer: 85/month (Year 1), growing to 110 (Year 3) as usage matures
4. Average call duration: 2.5 minutes
5. No regulatory headwinds requiring major product changes during projection period
6. No dominant competitor enters market with VC-backed pricing below cost

**Key Risks to Financial Model:**

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|-----------|
| Higher-than-modeled churn | Medium | High | Invest in CS; improve onboarding; build switching costs via integrations |
| AI API cost spike | Low | Medium | Multi-vendor strategy; cache common responses; optimize prompt token usage |
| TAM smaller than modeled | Low | High | Expand to adjacent verticals (dental, physical therapy) if beauty/salon proves limited |
| Sales velocity slower than modeled | Medium | High | Shift CAC mix toward lower-cost channels; extend seed runway |
| Competitor from existing POS player | Medium | Medium | Build deep vertical features they can't replicate fast; lock in partnerships |
